name: brainrender-napari

metadata.name: brainrender-napari
metadata.summary: A napari plugin to render BrainGlobe atlases and associated data as layers.

documentation:
# brainrender-napari

[![License BSD-3](https://img.shields.io/pypi/l/brainrender-napari.svg?color=green)](https://github.com/brainglobe/brainrender-napari/raw/main/LICENSE)
[![PyPI](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/brainrender-napari.svg?color=green)](https://pypi.org/project/brainrender-napari)
[![Python Version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/brainrender-napari.svg?color=green)](https://python.org)
[![tests](https://github.com/brainglobe/brainrender-napari/workflows/tests/badge.svg)](https://github.com/brainglobe/brainrender-napari/actions)
[![codecov](https://codecov.io/gh/brainglobe/brainrender-napari/branch/main/graph/badge.svg)](https://codecov.io/gh/brainglobe/brainrender-napari)
[![napari hub](https://img.shields.io/endpoint?url=https://api.napari-hub.org/shields/brainrender-napari)](https://napari-hub.org/plugins/brainrender-napari)

Visualisation and management of BrainGlobe atlases in napari.

----------------------------------

A napari plugin to visualise and manage BrainGlobe atlases. `brainrender-napari` aims to port the functionality of [`brainrender`](https://github.com/brainglobe/brainrender) to [`napari`](https://napari.org/stable/).
![add-region-brainrender-napari](https://github.com/brainglobe/brainrender-napari/assets/10500965/24fd3752-0ba7-4f47-aabf-5de22ff0f69b)

## Usage

Check out the ["Visualising an atlas in napari"](https://brainglobe.info/tutorials/visualise-atlas-napari.html) tutorial in the BrainGlobe documentation.

## Installation

We strongly recommend to use a virtual environment manager (like `conda` or `venv`). The installation instructions below will not specify the Qt backend for napari, and you will therefore need to install that separately. Please see [the `napari` installation instructions](https://napari.org/stable/tutorials/fundamentals/installation.html) for further advice on this.

You can install `brainrender-napari` via [pip]:

    pip install brainrender-napari



To install latest development version :

    pip install git+https://github.com/brainglobe/brainrender-napari.git

## Seeking help or contributing
We are always happy to help users of our tools, and welcome any contributions. If you would like to get in contact with us for any reason, please see the [contact page of our website](https://brainglobe.info/contact.html).

## License

Distributed under the terms of the [BSD-3] license,
"brainrender-napari" is free and open source software


## Acknowledgements

This [@napari] plugin was generated with [Cookiecutter] using [@napari]'s [cookiecutter-napari-plugin] template and the [Neuroinformatics Unit's template](https://github.com/neuroinformatics-unit/python-cookiecutter).

[Cookiecutter]: https://github.com/audreyr/cookiecutter
[@napari]: https://github.com/napari
[cookiecutter-napari-plugin]: https://github.com/napari/cookiecutter-napari-plugin
[BSD-3]: http://opensource.org/licenses/BSD-3-Clause
[file an issue]: https://github.com/brainglobe/brainrender-napari/issues
[tox]: https://tox.readthedocs.io/en/latest/
[pip]: https://pypi.org/project/pip/
[PyPI]: https://pypi.org/

modules.name: brainrender_napari.brainrender_manager_widget
documentation:
A napari widget to download and update atlases.

Available atlases are shown in a table view using the Qt model/view framework
[Qt Model/View framework](https://doc.qt.io/qt-6/model-view-programming.html)

classes.name: brainrender_napari.brainrender_manager_widget.BrainrenderManagerWidget

methods.name: brainrender_napari.brainrender_manager_widget.BrainrenderManagerWidget.__init__
documentation:
Instantiates the atlas viewer widget
and sets up coordinating connections

The purpose of this class is
* to hold atlas visualisation widgets for napari
* coordinate between these widgets and napari

classes.name: brainrender_napari.napari_atlas_representation.NapariAtlasRepresentation

methods.name: brainrender_napari.napari_atlas_representation.NapariAtlasRepresentation.__post_init__
documentation: Setup a custom QLabel tooltip and enable napari layer tooltips

methods.name: brainrender_napari.napari_atlas_representation.NapariAtlasRepresentation.add_to_viewer
documentation:
Adds the reference and annotation images as layers to the viewer.

The layers are connected to the mouse move callback to set tooltip.
The reference image's visibility is off, the annotation's is on.

methods.name: brainrender_napari.napari_atlas_representation.NapariAtlasRepresentation.add_structure_to_viewer
documentation:
Adds the mesh of a structure to the viewer.
The mesh will be rescaled to pixel space.

structure_name: the id or acronym of the structure.

methods.name: brainrender_napari.napari_atlas_representation.NapariAtlasRepresentation._add_mesh
documentation:
Helper function to add a mesh as a surface layer to the viewer.

mesh: the mesh to add
scale: List of scaling factors for each axis
name: name for the surface layer
color: RGB values (0-255) as a list to colour mesh with

methods.name: brainrender_napari.napari_atlas_representation.NapariAtlasRepresentation.add_additional_reference
documentation:
Adds a given additional reference as a layer to the viewer.
and connects it to the mouse move callback to set tooltip.

methods.name: brainrender_napari.napari_atlas_representation.NapariAtlasRepresentation._on_mouse_move
documentation:
Adapts the tooltip according to the cursor position.

The tooltip is only displayed if
* the viewer is in 2D display
* and the cursor is inside the annotation
* and the user has not switched off layer tooltips.

Note that layer, event input args are unused,
because all the required info is in
* the bg_atlas.structure_from_coords
* the (screen) cursor position
* the (napari) cursor position
Representation of a BG atlas as napari layers, in pixel space.

modules.name: brainrender_napari.brainrender_viewer_widget
documentation:
A napari widget to view atlases.

Locally available atlases are
shown in a table view using the Qt model/view framework
[Qt Model/View framework](https://doc.qt.io/qt-6/model-view-programming.html)

Users can add the atlas images/structures as layers to the viewer.

documentation:
Instantiates the atlas viewer widget
and sets up coordinating connections

documentation: Add given structure as napari atlas representation

methods.name: brainrender_napari.brainrender_viewer_widget.BrainrenderViewerWidget._on_additional_reference_requested
documentation: Add additional reference as napari atlas representation

methods.name: brainrender_napari.brainrender_viewer_widget.BrainrenderViewerWidget._on_atlas_selection_changed
documentation: Refreshes the structure view to match the changed atlas selection

methods.name: brainrender_napari.brainrender_viewer_widget.BrainrenderViewerWidget._on_add_atlas_requested
documentation: Add reference and annotation as napari atlas representation

The purpose of this class is
* to hold atlas visualisation widgets for napari
* coordinate between these widgets and napari

Customises the horizontal header data of model,

A table data model for atlases.

classes.name: brainrender_napari.data_models.structure_tree_model.StructureTreeModel

documentation: Build the structure tree given a list of structures.

documentation:
Provides read-only data for a given index if
intended for display, otherwise None.

methods.name: brainrender_napari.data_models.structure_tree_model.StructureTreeModel.rowCount
documentation: Returns the number of rows(i.e. children) of an item

methods.name: brainrender_napari.data_models.structure_tree_model.StructureTreeModel.columnCount
documentation: The number of columns of an item.

methods.name: brainrender_napari.data_models.structure_tree_model.StructureTreeModel.parent
documentation:
The first-column index of parent of the item
at a given index. Returns an empty index if the root,
or an invalid index, is passed.

methods.name: brainrender_napari.data_models.structure_tree_model.StructureTreeModel.index
documentation:
The index of the item at (row, column) with a given parent.
By default, the given parent is assumed to be the root.

Implementation of a read-only QAbstractItemModel to hold
the structure tree information provided by the Atlas API in a Qt Model

classes.name: brainrender_napari.widgets.atlas_manager_dialog.AtlasManagerDialog
documentation:
A modal dialog to ask users to confirm they'd like to download/update
the selected atlas, and warn them that it may be slow.

modules.name: brainrender_napari.widgets.atlas_manager_view
documentation:
The purpose of this file is to provide interactive table view to download
and update atlases. Users interacting with the table can request to
* download an atlas (double-click on row of a not-yet downloaded atlas)
* update an atlas (double-click on row of outdated local atlas)
They can also hover over an up-to-date local atlas and see that
it's up to date.

It is designed to be agnostic from the viewer framework by emitting signals
that any interested observers can connect to.

methods.name: brainrender_napari.widgets.atlas_manager_view.AtlasManagerView.__init__
documentation:
Initialises an atlas table view with latest atlas versions.

Also responsible for appearance, behaviour on selection, and
setting up signal-slot connections.

methods.name: brainrender_napari.widgets.atlas_manager_view.AtlasManagerView._on_download_atlas_confirmed
documentation: Downloads the currently selected atlas and signals this.

methods.name: brainrender_napari.widgets.atlas_manager_view.AtlasManagerView._on_update_atlas_confirmed
documentation: Updates the currently selected atlas and signals this.

methods.name: brainrender_napari.widgets.atlas_manager_view.AtlasManagerView.selected_atlas_name
documentation: A single place to get a valid selected atlas name.

methods.name: brainrender_napari.widgets.atlas_manager_view.AtlasManagerView._apply_in_thread
documentation: Calls `apply` on the given atlas in a separate thread.

methods.name: brainrender_napari.widgets.atlas_manager_view.AtlasManagerView.get_tooltip_text
documentation:
Returns the atlas name as a formatted string,
as well as instructions on how to interact with the atlas.

modules.name: brainrender_napari.widgets.atlas_viewer_view
documentation:
The purpose of this file is to provide an interactive table view
to request adding of atlas images. Users interacting it can request to
* add annotation and reference images (double-click on row of local atlas)
* add additional references (right-click on a row and select from  menu)

It is designed to be agnostic from the viewer framework by emitting signals
that interested observers can connect to.

methods.name: brainrender_napari.widgets.atlas_viewer_view.AtlasViewerView.__init__
documentation:
Initialises a table view with locally available atlas versions.

Also responsible for appearance, behaviour on selection, and
setting up signal-slot connections.

methods.name: brainrender_napari.widgets.atlas_viewer_view.AtlasViewerView.selected_atlas_name
documentation: A single place to get a valid selected atlas name.

methods.name: brainrender_napari.widgets.atlas_viewer_view.AtlasViewerView._on_context_menu_requested
documentation:
Returns a context menu with a list of additional references for the
currently selected atlas if the atlas has any. If the user selects one
of the additional references, this is signalled.

methods.name: brainrender_napari.widgets.atlas_viewer_view.AtlasViewerView._on_row_double_clicked
documentation:
Emits add_atlas_requested if the currently
selected atlas is available locally.

methods.name: brainrender_napari.widgets.atlas_viewer_view.AtlasViewerView._on_current_changed
documentation: Emits a signal with the newly selected atlas name

methods.name: brainrender_napari.widgets.atlas_viewer_view.AtlasViewerView.get_tooltip_text
documentation:
Returns the atlas metadata as a formatted string,
as well as instructions on how to interact with the atlas.

functions.name: brainrender_napari.utils.load_user_data.read_atlas_metadata_from_file
documentation: Reads atlas metadata stored in a `.json` in the BrainGlobe directory.

functions.name: brainrender_napari.utils.load_user_data.read_atlas_structures_from_file
documentation: Reads structure info from a '.json' in the BrainGlobe directory.

functions.name: brainrender_napari.utils.formatting.format_atlas_name
documentation:
Format an atlas name nicely.
Assumes input in the form of atlas_name_in_snake_case_RESOLUTIONum,
e.g. allen_mouse_100um

modules.name: brainrender_napari.widgets.structure_view
documentation:
The purpose of this file is to provide interactive item,
model and view classes for the structures that form part of an atlas.
The view is only visible if the atlas is downloaded.

classes.name: brainrender_napari.widgets.structure_view.StructureTreeItem
documentation: A class to hold items in a tree model.

The index of the item at (row, column) with a given parent.

methods.name: brainrender_napari.widgets.structure_view.StructureView.refresh
documentation:
Updates the structure tree view with the currently selected atlas.
The view is only visible if the selected atlas has been downloaded.
Resets the current index either way.
